observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a) the Municipal Accountant to cause the dropping from the books the disposed property worth P3,294,062.39; and
b) the GSO to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property for all identified unserviceable properties of the Municipality and dispose the same in accordance with existing regulations.
observations.managementAction: No actions were taken by the Municipality.
observations.reason: Several lockdowns implemented by the Municipality due to Corona Virus Disease 2019 (COVID 19) pandemic affected the implementation of this recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. OIC - General Services Officer to conduct physical count of all its PPE
including its Land and Land Improvements and submit to COA their
corresponding RPCPPE;
b. Municipal Accountant and the OIC - General Services Officer to specifically
identify and provide details of the property totaling P78,397,105.23 in the
RPCPPE taken from old Municipality records;
c. OIC - General Services Officer to assign Property Number to each PPE item,
tag the PPE items with the property stickers and indicate the same in the
RPCPPE to facilitate their proper identification; and
d. OIC - General Services Officer to use the prescribed RPCPPE form under
Section 57 of the MNGAS for LGUs, Volume II.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the necessary adjusting journal entries to adjust the RPT and SET
Receivables balance and their corresponding Deferred Income accounts to include
the accrued and uncollected real property taxes from prior years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. submit to the Audit Team all necessary documents such as Delivery Receipts,
Acknowledgement Receipts, Contractor’s Statement of Work Accomplished,
Certificate of Inspection and Acceptance and other pertinent documents to prove
the validity of the recorded Accounts Payable, otherwise make the necessary
adjustments in the books of accounts; and
b. ensure that all relevant supporting documents are attached to the claims and
provide complete information such as date to establish proof of actual delivery of
goods, services and infrastructure projects.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:
a. cause the immediate reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the general fund
of the Accounts Payable totaling P12,639,285.22 which remained outstanding for
more than two years and were not supported with complete documentation; and
b. inform in writing the concerned suppliers and contractors about their unclaimed
retention money with the advice that the amount will be reverted to the
unappropriated surplus of the general fund of the Municipality should proper
claim be not made within reasonable period.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
prepare the JV for the restoration of cash equivalent of the unreleased checks and
recognition of the appropriate payable/liability accounts at the end of the year and in
addition, reverse the entries made in the ensuing year, in compliance with COA
GAFMIS Circular Letter No. 2002-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Treasurer to submit
to him, for review and approval, a report outlining the specific and time-bound
strategies and actions her Office plans to pursue to enable the Municipality to resolve
the issue on delinquent taxes, taking into consideration the legal remedies provided
under Sections 258 and 260 of RA 7160.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct
the LFC to analyze the trend in the collection of tax revenues in the previous years
and determine the amount of income which has reasonable assurance of collectivity
and use the data as inputs or benchmark in the preparation of Annual Budget for the
ensuing fiscal year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee (LFC)
to adopt measures to eliminate the cash overdraft by exercising diligence, prudence,
and proper control in the disposition of the Municipality’s funds and limit
expenditures within the total income realized and collected during the year and
reduce unnecessary operating expenditures until the cash overdraft is eliminated.

We also reiterated our recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local
Finance Committee to make a thorough analysis of the tax and non-tax revenues
collected and realized for the previous years and use the results of such analysis as
basis in the preparation of an attainable Annual Budget for the ensuing fiscal year.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
a. General Services Officer (GSO) to expedite the disposal of the unserviceable
properties amounting to P3,352,280.71 by submitting to COA Auditor request for
disposal of the unserviceable properties together with the IIRUP, Appraisal Report
and other supporting documents and upon disposal, direct the Municipal
Accountant to draw a Journal Entry Voucher to derecognize in the books their
carrying value; and
b. The Municipal Accountant to derecognize the disposed property amounting to
P3,331,362.39 in the books of the Municipality.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the LDRRM Officer to promptly
investigate and address all issues which prevented the implementation of the PPAs
funded by the LDRRMF so that the desired goals and objectives of reducing disaster
risk and enhancing disaster preparedness and response capabilities may be
optimally achieved in the ensuing years.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a) the Municipal Accountant and OIC - General Services Officer (GSO) to specifically identify the property presented in lump sum amount totaling P129,728,476.38 in the books and RPCPPE; and
b) the GSO to assign Property Number to every PPE item, indicate the same in the RPCPPE and place property tag on the PPE to facilitate their proper identification.
observations.managementAction: Identified some property presented in lump sum amount in the books and RPCPPE; Newly acquired PPEs were assigned with property numbers.
observations.reason: 17.88 per cent of the total inventory were presented in lump sum amount in the RPCPPE. Several lockdowns implemented by the Municipality due to Corona Virus Disease 2019 (COVID 19) pandemic affected the implementation of this recommendation.

observations.recommendation: We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee to analyze the trend in the collection of tax revenues for the last three years and determine the amount of income which has reasonable assurance of collectivity and use the data as inputs or benchmark in the preparation of 2022 Annual Budget. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer to exercise diligence, prudence, and proper control in the disposition of the Municipality’s funds and limit expenditures within the total actual income realized.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Committee studied the trend in the collection of tax revenues and the Local Finance Team monitored expenses in relation with the actual income realized.
observations.reason: Corona Virus Disease 2019 (COVID 19) pandemic greatly affected the expenditures and disbursements of the Municipality in 2021.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a) Municipal Accountant to properly record the purchases of welfare goods under the account Welfare Goods for Distribution and the distributed portion to Welfare Goods Expenses and maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for all welfare goods; and
b) Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO) to maintain Stock Cards to record the purchases and issuances of welfare goods and submit Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) and Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) as basis of the City Accountant in recording issued items under account Welfare Goods Expenses.
observations.managementAction: Purchases of welfare goods were taken up as inventory and recorded as expense only upon issuance. Stock Cards were maintained by the MSWDO. Issuances of these items were supported with Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) and reported monthly to the Accounting Office thru the preparation and submission of monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI) as basis of the City Accountant in recording issued items under account Welfare Goods Expenses.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to coordinate with responsible officials to facilitate the immediate submission to the Commission on Audit of the lacking documents and/or correction of deficiencies noted in audit to prevent suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions. Henceforth, ensure that all required supporting documents are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers before payment to facilitate the review of the validity and propriety of the disbursements.
observations.managementAction: Required supporting documents were submitted by the concerned Municipal Officials. Henceforth, ensure that all supporting documents are attached to the Disbursement Vouchers of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to take up the office supplies account and to recognize expense only upon issuance. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the District Supervisor, require the District Property Custodian to submit to the Accounting Office the RSMI together with the Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) of all office supplies issued to facilitate the recording of office supplies expense in the books.
observations.managementAction: RSMI together with the RIS were submitted by the District Property Custodian to the Accounting Office.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee to allocate appropriation and funds to recoup the amount borrowed by the General Fund from the Trust Fund to ensure that trust funds are available for the specific purpose for which the trust fund was created or the funds received. We further recommended that if the preceding recommendation is not legally feasible, the Municipal Mayor and other responsible officials explain in writing to the COA Audit Team, for further evaluation, the circumstances behind the utilization of the trust funds amounting to P3,327,283.06 for purposes other than for what said funds were established, including a report on who are accountable for the apparent irregular or illegal expenditures of trust funds.
observations.managementAction: First recommendation was adopted by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We strongly reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances, specifically to refrain from granting additional cash advances to any official or employee without liquidating first his/her prior cash advance and to ensure that all cash advances are liquidated at year-end.
observations.managementAction: Guidelines in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances were complied by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to increase the amount of fidelity bond of the subject accountable officers to cover the amount of cash advances granted to them pursuant to Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, otherwise, limit the amount of their cash advances up to the maximum cash accountability of their fidelity bond.
observations.managementAction: Fidelity bond of the accountable officers were increased.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to refrain from consolidating multiple claims or DVs in one check regardless of whether or not the DVs pertained to the claims of one and the same payee to ensure a more effective and efficient tracing, monitoring and examination of individual DVs and supporting documents.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant ensured that only one DV was paid per check.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a) the Municipal Accountant and OIC - General Services Officer (GSO) to
specifically identify the property presented in lump sum amount totaling
P129,728,476.38 in the books and RPCPPE; and
b) the GSO to assign property number to every PPE, indicate the same in the
RPCPPE and place property tag on the PPE to facilitate their proper
identification.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a) the Municipal Accountant to cause the dropping from the books the disposed
property worth P3,294,062.39; and
b) the GSO to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable
Property for all identified unserviceable properties of the Municipality and
dispose the same in accordance with existing regulations.

We reiterated our recommendations that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance
Committee to analyze the trend in the collection of tax revenues for the last three years
and determine the amount of income which has reasonable assurance of collectivity
and use the data as inputs or benchmark in the preparation of Annual Budget for the
ensuing fiscal year.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer,
Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer to exercise diligence, prudence, and
proper control in the disposition of the Municipality’s funds and limit expenditures
within the total income realized and collected during the year to avoid incurrence of
cash overdraft.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the:
a) Municipal Accountant to properly record the purchases of welfare goods under the
account Welfare Goods for Distribution and the distributed portion to Welfare Goods
Expenses. She should also maintain Supplies Ledger Cards for all welfare goods; and
b) Municipal Social Worker and Development Office (MSWDO) to maintain Stock Cards
to record the purchases and issuances of welfare goods. The issuances of these
items should be supported with the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) and reported monthly to the
Accounting Office thru the preparation and submission of monthly Report of Supplies
and Materials Issued (RSMI) as basis of the City Accountant in recording issued items
under account Welfare Goods Expenses.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to
coordinate with responsible officials to facilitate the immediate submission to the
Commission on Audit of the lacking documents and/or correction of deficiencies noted in
audit to prevent suspension and/or disallowance of the transactions. Henceforth,
ensure that all required supporting documents are attached to the Disbursement
Vouchers before payment to facilitate the review of the validity and propriety of the
disbursements.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to take up
the office supplies procured under the inventory account and to recognize expense only
upon distribution or consumption based on the Report of Supplies and Materials Issued
(RSMI) duly submitted by the District Property Custodian.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor, thru the District Supervisor, require the
District Property Custodian to submit to the Accounting Office the RSMI together with the
Requisition and Issue Slips (RIS) of all office supplies issued to facilitate the recording of
office supplies expense in the books.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee to
allocate appropriation and funds to recoup the amount borrowed by the General Fund
from the Trust Fund to ensure that trust funds are available for the specific purpose for
which the trust was created or the funds received.

We further recommended that if the preceding recommendation is not legally feasible,
the Municipal Mayor and other responsible officials explain in writing to the COA Audit
Team, for further evaluation, the circumstances behind the utilization of the trust funds
amounting to P3,327,283.06 for purposes other than for what said funds were
established, which was a violation of Section 4 (3) of Presidential Decree 1445,
including a report on who are accountable for the apparent irregular or illegal
expenditures of trust funds.

We strongly reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor
direct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the guidelines in the granting,
utilization and liquidation of cash advances, specifically to refrain from granting
additional cash advances to any official or employee without liquidating first his/her
prior cash advance and to ensure that all cash advances are liquidated at year-end.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to increase
the amount of fidelity bond of the subject accountable officers to cover the amount of
cash advances granted to them pursuant to Treasury Circular No. 02-2009, otherwise,
limit the amount of their cash advances up to the maximum cash accountability of their
fidelity bond.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to refrain
from consolidating multiple claims or DVs in one check regardless of whether or not
the DVs pertained to the claims of one and the same payee to ensure a more effective and
efficient tracing, monitoring and examination of individual DVs and supporting
documents.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: • the Municipal Accountant and the OIC - General Services Officer exert more effort to reconcile the difference of P57,134,999.62 between the accounting records of PPE and RPCPPE; • the General Services Officer to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report on Unserviceable Property for all identified unserviceable properties of the Municipality and cause its disposal in accordance with the existing regulation; and • the General Services Officer to provide details of the property totaling P80,484,692.23 taken up in the RPCPPE in lump sum as amount taken from damaged old municipal record.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the OIC - General Services Officer reconciled the existing differences
observations.reason: Not Actions had not Implemented been taken by the GSO on this recommendation. Records pertaining to these PPE were not maintained by both the Accounting and General Services Offices.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee to analyze the trend in the collection of tax revenues for the last three years and determine the amount of income which has reasonable assurance of collectivity and use the data as inputs or benchmark in the preparation of Annual Budget for the ensuing fiscal year.
observations.managementAction: The Local Finance Team monitored the expenses in relation with the actual income realized.
observations.reason: The presence of Corona Virus Disease 2019 (COVID 19) pandemic greatly affected the expenditures and disbursements of the Municipality in 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development Council, Acting Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to determine the viability of the projects to be funded out of 20 per cent Development Fund before its inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan to ensure its full implementation.
observations.managementAction: The unexpended Development Fund for 2016 to 2019 were reprogrammed for the COVID 19 project, programs and activities in April 2020 and were fully utilized at year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the guidelines in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and remind all the concerned officials/employees to immediately settle their outstanding cash advances after their purpose had been served and to fully liquidate them at the end of the year. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to ensure that no additional cash advances shall be granted to any official/employee unless his or her previous cash advance/s are fully settled.
observations.managementAction: Instructions has been given by the Municipal Mayor to the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances and the latter promised compliance.
observations.reason: There were still outstanding cash advances at the end of the year due to non-completion of the activity during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor exercise prudence in the formulation of procedures and guidelines that will set the parameters, conditions and limitations on the grant of donation/financial assistance taking into account the need and circumstances of the applicants and the purpose and nature of their requests.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality had started the formulation of policies and procedures on the granting of donation which is still for approval of the Municipal Mayor and will be forwarded to the Sangguniang Bayan for enactment.
observations.reason: The municipal hall underwent several lockdowns in 2020 due to the spread of COVID 19, thus hindering the normal operations of the government.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person to provide disclosures in the GAD Accomplishment Report regarding the reasons for the deviations, as well as the factors that have hindered the implementation of the programs/projects/activities embodied in the related Municipality’s GAD Plan and Budget. We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned department heads to ensure the optimum utilization of the GAD Budget by fully implementing the identified GAD programs, projects and activities.
observations.managementAction: The COVID 19 outbreak has greatly affected the activities enumerated in the GAD Plan for CY 2020. Reprogramming of projects, programs and activities were undertaken to augment the funds needed for COVID 19-related expenses. The same were disclosed in a separate report submitted by the Budget Office. Moreover, there was optimum utilization of the GAD budget in 2020.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to immediately remit all taxes withheld to the BIR. Henceforth, it is recommended that withheld taxes be closely remitted to the BIR.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant updated the remittances of taxes withheld to the BIR.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the Municipal Accountant: • to review the composition of the Accounts Payable to determine the composition of valid claims and to ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with the required and necessary documents; • to draw Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the claim debited to Donations accounts instead of Accounts Payable; and • to revert to the unappropriated surplus all accounts payable which have been outstanding for two years or more and for which no valid claims have been established in compliance to Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The necessary adjusting entries has been taken up in the books in June 30, 2020 under Journal Entry Voucher No. 4561. All accounts payable which have been outstanding for two years or more were reverted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: • Municipal Accountant to properly record the purchases of supplies, materials, equipment and relief goods under the account Welfare Goods for Distribution and the distributed portion to Welfare Goods Expenses. • Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office (MSWDO) to maintain Stock Cards to record the purchases and issuances of welfare goods. The issuances of these items should be supported with the Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) and reported monthly to the Accounting Office thru the preparation and submission of monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI). • Municipal Accountant and MSWDO to comply with the accounting and reporting guidelines under COA Circular Nos. 2014-002 and 2015-009 dated April 15, 2014 and December 1, 2015, respectively, to ensure the correctness and reliability of the financial reporting of the Municipality.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant and the MSWDO complied partially. Supplies ledger cards for relief goods treated as inventory items were maintained. The concerned department heads had partially complied with the accounting and reporting guidelines on the utilization of the LDRRMF.
observations.reason: There were inconsistencies in recording goods.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
• the Municipal Accountant and the OIC - General Services Officer exert more effort
to reconcile the difference of P57,134,999.62 existing between the accounting
records of PPE and RPCPPE;
• the General Services Officer to prepare the Inventory and Inspection Report on
Unserviceable Property for all identified unserviceable properties of the
Municipality and cause its disposal in accordance with the existing regulation;
and
• the General Services Officer to provide details of the property totaling
P80,484,692.23 taken up in the RPCPPE in lump sum as amount taken from
damaged old municipal record.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local Finance Committee to
analyze the trend in the collection of tax revenues for the last three years and
determine the amount of income which has reasonable assurance of collectivity and
use the data as inputs or benchmark in the preparation of Annual Budget for the
ensuing fiscal year.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer,
Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer to exercise diligence, prudence, and
proper control in the disposition of the Municipality’s funds and limit expenditures
within the total income realized and collected during the year to avoid incurrence of
cash overdraft.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Development
Council, Acting Municipal Planning and Development Coordinator and Municipal
Engineer to determine the viability of the projects to be funded out of 20 per cent
Development Fund before its inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan to ensure its
full implementation.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to
strictly adhere to the guidelines in granting, utilization and liquidation of cash
advances and remind all the concerned officials/employees to immediately settle their
outstanding cash advances after their purpose had been served and to fully
liquidate them at the end of the year.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor further direct the Municipal
Accountant to ensure that no additional cash advances shall be granted to any
official/employee unless his or her previous cash advance/s are fully settled.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor exercise prudence in the disbursements
for donation to help address the cash deficit of the Municipality and to initiate the
formulation of policies, procedures and guidelines that will set the parameters,
conditions and limitations on the grant of donation/financial assistance taking into
account the need and circumstances of the applicants and the purpose and nature of
their requests.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the GAD Focal Person to provide
disclosures in the GAD Accomplishment Report regarding the reasons for the
deviations, as well as the factors that have hindered the implementation of the
programs/projects/activities embodied in the Municipality’s GAD Plan and Budget.
We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the concerned department
heads to ensure the optimum utilization of GAD Budget by fully implementing the
identified GAD programs, projects and activities.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct both the Municipal Treasurer and
the Accountant to immediately remit all taxes due to the BIR. Henceforth, it is
recommended that withheld taxes due the BIR be remitted on or before the 10th day of
the month following the month in which withholding was made as required under BIR
Regulation No. 2-98 to prevent incurrence of surcharges and penalties for late
remittances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor requires the Municipal Accountant:
• to review the composition of the Accounts payable to determine valid claims and to
ensure that all recorded payables are duly supported with the required and
necessary documents;
• to draw Journal Entry Voucher to adjust the claim debited to Donations accounts
instead of Accounts Payable; and
• to revert to the unappropriated surplus all accounts payable which have been
outstanding for two years or more and for which no valid claims have been
established in compliance to Section 98 of Presidential Decree No. 1445.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
• Municipal Accountant to properly record the purchases of supplies, materials,
equipment and relief goods under the account Welfare Goods for Distribution
and the distributed portion to Welfare Goods Expenses.
• Municipal Social Welfare Development Officer (MSWDO) to maintain Stock Cards
to record the purchases and issuances of relief goods procured out of LDRRMF.
Issuances of these items should be supported with Requisition Issue Slip and
reported monthly to the Accounting Office thru the preparation and submission
of Monthly Report of Supplies and Materials Issued (RSMI); and
• Municipal Accountant and MSWDO to comply with the accounting and reporting
guidelines on the utilization of the LDRRMF prescribed under COA Circular Nos.
2014-002 and 2015-009 dated April 15, 2014 and December 1, 2015, respectively,
to ensure the correctness and reliability of the financial reporting of the
Municipality.
We reiterated our recommendation that direct the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Services Officer to exert efforts to analyze and reconcile the Accounting Records.
observations.managementAction: The Inventory, Appraisal and Disposal Committee was made functional. Thorough analysis of the PPEs was conducted and proper reconciliation was made with GSO records. Such records are vital to be used as reference on the Accounting Records. The Inventory and the Disposal Committee members convened several times to take action on this. The Inventory and adjusting entries to be made, hence several accounts remained unreconciled.
observations.reason: Due to absence of GSO records, the Accounting Office encountered difficulties in the reconciliation of PPE accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to reconcile the accounting records with that of the Treasury and make the necessary adjustment to reflect the correct balance of the RPT and SET Receivables and Deferred RPT and SET Income based on the certified list of taxpayers submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.
observations.managementAction: Thorough analysis of the RPT and SET receivables by the Municipal Accountant. It was discovered that there has been an error in the recording of advance payments. Proper adjusting entries has been made to verified amounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the: a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Daily Cash Position Report to serve as guide in certifying that funds are available to ensure that all disbursements have cash back-up; and b. Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer to exercise diligence, prudence, and proper control in the disposition of the Municipality’s funds and limit expenditures within the total income realized and collected during the year to avoid incurrence of cash overdraft.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer submits a Cash Position Report to the Municipal Mayor. The Local Chief Executive wanted to ensure that basic services are directly given to Kawit constituents. The Finance Committee was activated and made functional to discuss income generating activities as well as cost-saving procedures.
observations.reason: Cash deficit still exists in the General Fund due to non-realization of income generating activities as well as targeted revenues.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Treasurer revisit the tax collections in 2018 and review the computation made starting on the top taxpayers in order to collect from those with under-assessed or erroneously computed taxes. Otherwise, Notices of Charge will be issued for any under-collection found in audit. Henceforth, the Municipal Treasurer should install effective controls to ensure correct imposition or computation of penalties and discounts in the payment of taxes.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer issued Notices to inform concerned taxpayers requesting for additional payments to settle the unpaid/errors in computation of interests/discounts given, to prevent the issuance by the Audit Team of Notice of Charge against concerned Collecting Officers in the amount of P250,488.14. The Software System of the Municipal Treasurer's Office was adjusted to ensure correctness in computation of interest and penalties.
observations.reason: Due to voluminous workload revisiting tax collections for 2018 and revisiting tax collections for 2018 will be tedious.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct both the Municipal Treasurer and the Accountant to immediately remit all taxes due to the BIR. Henceforth, withheld taxes due the BIR be remitted on or before the 10th day of the month following the month in which withholding was made as required under BIR Regulation No. 2-98 to prevent incurrence of surcharges and penalties for late remittances.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting Office ensures that it has religiously remitted the monthly payments to the Bureau of Internal Revenue. The Municipal Accountant was instructed by the Local Chief Executive to analyze the composition of the taxes withheld in prior year/s and cause the immediate remittance of the same to the Bureau of Internal Revenue.
observations.reason: Taxes withheld in prior years which remained unremitted are for further verification as to its composition. Necessary adjustment/s will be made if necessary.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor effect the optimum utilization of the MDRRMF in order to strengthen the Municipality’s disaster preparedness and the MDRRMO to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of Disaster Risk Reduction Fund to facilitate proper monitoring and evaluation of the utilization of the MDRRMF.
observations.managementAction: The LDRRM Council convenes every month. As such, a Monthly Sources and Utilization Report is prepared for close monitoring of projects that were completed and activities that were accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineering Office and the BAC Secretariat to furnish the Audit Team with copies of approved contracts and all the documents forming part thereof within five days from their execution as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 to facilitate prompt review and evaluation of the contracts and early communication to management of the deficiencies, if any.
observations.managementAction: A new set of BAC members, Secretariat and TWG were appointed by the Municipal Mayor. Copies of some of the contracts were forwarded to the Audit Team within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Incomplete documentation hindered the timely submission of approved contracts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the BAC Secretariat comply with the required postings of the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and Contracts in the PhilGEPS and in the website of the Municipality, if any, within three and 15 calendar days, respectively, from their issuance.
observations.managementAction: A new set of BAC Secretariat members and TWG were appointed by the Municipal Mayor. Notice to Award, Notice to Proceed and Contracts pertaining to projects under the 20 per cent Development Fund were posted where applicable.
observations.reason: The previous BAC Secretariat was not able to post Contracts, Notice to Award, Notice to Proceed pertaining to some projects under the 20 per cent Development Fund.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Budget Officer and the BAC Chairman ensure that the required PPMP and APP for Infrastructure Projects are prepared and approved before any procurement of goods, services and civil works is undertaken to properly plan and monitor the priority projects of the Municipality for a particular budget year.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Budget Officer and the BAC Chairman ensured that the PPMP and APP are prepared. The Municipal Budget Officer was able to prepare a PPMP and APP but some activities were lacking.
observations.reason: The Municipal Budget Officer was able to prepare a PPMP and APP but lacked some activities. The BAC Chairman was not able to prepare a comprehensive PPMP and APP.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct that the amount of fidelity bond be increased to P3,500,000.00 as provided for under the Revised Schedule of Premium Rates to ensure that the government is duly indemnified in case of loss of funds.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer has increased the amount of fidelity bond of Ms. Ocava.

observations.recommendation:
We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Municipal Mayor direct
the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal General Service Officer to exert
efforts to analyze and reconcile the discrepancy of P53,166,748.87 between the
accounting records and the RCPPE and make the adjustments where necessary.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Accountant to
reconcile the accounting records with that of the Treasury and make the
necessary adjustment to reflect the correct balance of the RPT and SET
Receivables and Deferred RPT and SET Income based on the certified list of
taxpayers submitted by the Municipal Treasurer.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the:
a. Municipal Treasurer to prepare the Daily Cash Position Report to serve as
guide in certifying that funds are available to ensure that all
disbursements have cash back-up; and to adopt strategies to increase
revenues/collections;
b. Municipal Budget Officer, Municipal Accountant, and Municipal Treasurer
to exercise diligence, prudence, and proper control in the disposition of
the Municipality’s funds and limit expenditures within the total income
realized and collected during the year to avoid incurrence of cash
overdraft.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Treasurer to
issue a notice to concerned taxpayers requesting for additional payments to
settle the unpaid/errors in computation of interests on delinquent real property
taxes or refund the excess discounts given, to prevent the issuance by the Audit
Team of Notice of Charge against concerned Collecting Officers in the amount of
P250,488.14.

We also recommended that the Municipal Treasurer revisit the tax collections in
2018 and review the computation made starting on the top taxpayers in order to
collect from those with under-assessed or erroneously computed taxes.
Otherwise, Notices of Charge will be issued for any under-collection found in
audit.

Henceforth, we recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Treasurer to install effective controls to ensure correct imposition or computation
of penalties or discounts in the payment of taxes.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct both the Municipal Treasurer
and the Accountant to effect the immediate remittance of the taxes due to the BIR.
Henceforth, withheld taxes due the BIR be remitted on or before the tenth (10th)
day of the month following the month in which withholding was made as required
under BIR Regulation No. 2-98 to prevent incurrence of surcharges and penalties
for late remittances.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor effect the optimum utilization of the
MDRRMF in order to strengthen the Municipality’s disaster preparedness and
direct the MDRRMO to submit the Monthly Report on Sources and Utilization of
Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund to facilitate proper monitoring and
evaluation of the utilization of the MDRRMF.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal Engineering
Office and the BAC Secretariat to furnish the Audit Team with copies of approved
contracts and all the documents forming part thereof within five days from their
execution as prescribed under COA Circular No. 2009-001 dated February 12,
2009, to facilitate prompt review and evaluation of the contracts and early
communication to management of the deficiencies, if any.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the BAC Secretariat to comply
with the required postings of the Notice of Award, Notice to Proceed and Contract
in the PhilGEPS and in the website of the Municipality, if any, within three and 15
calendar days, respectively, from their issuance.

We recommended that the Municipal Budget Officer and the BAC Chairman
ensure that the required PPMP and APP for Infrastructure Projects are prepared
and approved before any procurement of goods, services and civil works is
undertaken to properly plan and monitor the priority projects of the Municipality
for a particular budget year.

We recommended that the amount of fidelity bond of Ms. Ocava be increased to
P3,500,000.00 as provided for under the Revised Schedule of Premium Rates of
the above-mentioned Treasury Circular to ensure that the government is duly
indemnified in case of losses of funds.

observations.managementAction:
Acting Property Officer ensures that the ARE and/or PAR are issued and/or PAR are issued for all PPE items issued to actual recipients and/or end-users and renewed every three years;
The acting Property Officer prepares the ARE/PAR to transfer the items accountable to any retiring employee prior to issuance of clearance.
The acting Property Officer assures that if there will be any demolished infra asset, it shall be reported to the Municipal Accountant's Office for proper dropping from the books of account.
After payment of the bidded unserviceable equipment, it is dropped from the books of account;
This is religiously observed prior to issuance of clearance to outgoing officials
Some items have been issued the necessary Deed of Donations
Ms. Joyce Cavas has been transferred to the General Services Office to help in the facilitate the reconciliation of PPE accounts. She has also been designated as the Secretrary to the Inventory Committee.
The Inventory Committee convened several times to evaluate and come up with the appraised value of unserviceable motor vehicles. After which the necessary procedures for public auction will be conducted.
observations.reason: Partial implemenation of the audit recommendation is ongoing. The Inventory Committee still cannot come up with the appraised value of unserviceable motor vehicles. Officials did not provide explanation on their partial implementation. Incomplete documentations. Lack of documents.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. the concerned Municipal official to work out and see to it that Titles of the purchased and donated parcels of land are transferred in the name of the Municipal Government of Kawit to establish the Municipality’s absolute ownership thereof.
b. the Budget Officer to include in the preparation of budget the allocation for titling of land owned by the Municipality; and
c. the Municipal Accountant to book up the parcels of land which were already transferred to the Municipality by way of donation or deed of sale.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Assessor conducted a thorough investigation and analysis of the Land claimed to be owned by the Municipality of Kawit. It was discovered that the land used by the High School has been titled under the name of DepEd. Coordination by the Municipal Accountant, Municipal Assessor, Property Officer and Municipal Treasurer is necessary to ensure the correct recording of the parcels of land in the books of accounts. The Budget Officer coordinated with the Municipal Assessor to have a list of land owned by the Municipality of Kawit. The Municipal Assessor coordinated with the Mayor's Office regarding those lands that were donated for proper issuance of Deed of Sale under said Office.
observations.reason: The Assessor conducted a coordinated investigation. The Municipal Assessor coordinated with the Mayor's Office. The Budget Officer is waiting for the projected amount to be incurred in the titling of land owned by the Municipality of Kawit.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that:
a. the Acting Supply Officer to consolidate RIS on a weekly basis the RIS using the SSMI and submit to the Accounting Office for recording of issued supplies and materials;
b. the Municipal Accountant to compute the cost of supplies issued and ending inventory based on the SSMI and to prepare a journal entry voucher (JEV) to record the expenditures using appropriate expenditures accounts; and
c. the Acting Supply Officer to conduct a complete physical count of all inventory items regularly at the end of every semester and render a report thereon within the prescribed deadline and reconcile the records of the Supply Office with the Accounting Office on a per category basis.
observations.managementAction: The Acting Supply Officer consolidated RIS on a monthly basis and was instructed to consolidate the RIS on a weekly basis instead of monthly. The corresponding Journal Entry Voucher has been made to record the expenditures account based on the report on ending inventory submitted by the different departments/offices. The Acting Supply Officer conducted the complete physical count of all inventory items after which a copy has been submitted to the Accounting Office. The Accounting Office on the other hand, prepares a report on newly purchased/acquired PPEs for proper reconciliation with GSO.
observations.reason: JEV preparation is still ongoing. Partial consolidation now done weekly but some reconciliation and documentation still pending.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to closely monitor the cash balance of each fund and enjoin the Municipal Treasurer from certifying availability when no cash is available to avoid cash deficits. Otherwise, appropriate sanctions maybe imposed against responsible officials for existence of cash deficits. We also recommended that trust fund be utilized only for the specific purpose for which it was intended as required under Section 4(3) of PD No. 1445.
observations.managementAction: The Trust Fund is always utilized only for which purpose it was intended for. A certificate is first issued prior to conduct of bidding procedures. The Municipal Treasurer has been instructed to monitor the availability of funds.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant look into the difference in balances of the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables accounts and make the necessary adjustments thereon at their correct balances. We also recommended that henceforth, the Accountant shall ensure that both accounts carry the same balances from the beginning of the year until year end.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant conducted the thorough analysis of RPT Receivables and set RPT Receivables. Proper Journal Entries has also been made.

observations.recommendation: We recommended the Honorable Mayor to direct the HRMO and other concerned officials to immediately review the work description of each permanent employee and assess if there is really a need to hire the services of consultants. The Municipality should maximize the worth of its in-house employees before resorting to hiring of consultants. Consultants should only be hired for special projects requiring highly technical and professional expertise as provided by IRR of Republic Act No. 9184 and Section 1(a) of CSC Resolution No. 021480 as circularized under Memorandum Circular No. 24 s 2002 for Consultancy Services.
observations.managementAction: The functions of consultants have been scrutinized and checked whether it only duplicates the duties of some permanent employees. The number of hired consultants have been reduced since July 2018. The municipality lacks the needed manpower that would require highly technical expertise. Creation of additional position would be impossible.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to properly record and classify financial transactions in accordance with COA Circular No. 2015-009 to ensure that financial statements present fairly the financial position of the Municipality and that the effects of transactions are faithfully represented.
observations.managementAction: The donations account has been thoroughly analyzed and it was observed that the problem lies on the classification of accounts. For CY 2018, proper classification of accounts has been made. If the expense benefited other entities or individual not directly connected to the municipality, then that would be classified under donations. If the expense was incurred to support operations of the municipality, then it was classified under corresponding expense account.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:
a. Project Implementer as well as the Municipal Planning and Development Officer to properly plan/screen the programs and projects before its inclusion in the priority list of PPAs to be undertaken by the Municipality to avoid return of funds due to non-implementation of the planned PPA.
b. BUB Focal Person to closely monitor the implementation of the BUB projects so that the optimum goal of inclusive growth and poverty reduction could be fully attained.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO has been designated as the focal person for all BUB projects, as well as projects under the Trust Fund. She has religiously monitored the status of each project by discussing it with the corresponding implementing department/office. She is also responsible in the monthly submission of statement of receipts and disbursement prepared by the Municipal Accountant. Through this, both department heads are able to update and reconcile their records.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:
• Municipal Engineer to ensure the full utilization of the 20% DF for its intended projects and be guided always by the afore-cited Joint Memorandum Circular.
• Municipal Planning and Development Officer (MPDO) to closely monitor the implementation of the development projects programmed under the 20% DF to ensure the optimum utilization of the fund and desirable socio-economic and development outcomes.
observations.managementAction: The MPDO and Municipal Engineer has a close coordination to ensure that the PPA's are still implemented feasible. For CY 2017, the PPA's have been utilized 100% as of December 2018. The MPDO is responsible to closely monitor the implementation of the development projects programmed under the 20% DF to ensure the optimum utilization of the fund and desirable socio-economic and development outcomes.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Sangguniang Bayan in coordination with the Solid Waste Management Board enact ordinances to carry out the requirements of R.A. No. 9003, specifically the imposition of fines and penalties for violators to strengthen the implementation of solid waste management plan throughout the Municipality. We likewise recommended that the Municipal Mayor require strict monitoring of compliance with the requirements of R.A. No. 9003 especially at the barangay level.
observations.managementAction: The Sangguniang Bayan in coordination with the Solid Waste Management Board enact ordinances requiring waste segregation, the imposition of fines and penalties 'No Segregation No Garbage Collection Policy'. Penalties has also been imposed. Such ordinances has been printed on tarpaulins to inform the public.
observations.reason: The Municipal Council already submitted the Ordinance to the Province but it was not yet approved.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer find ways and means to address the cash deficits the municipality had been experiencing for several years now by exercising diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of government funds.
observations.managementAction: No action had been taken in this recommendation
observations.reason: Insufficient cash balance of the General Fund. Cash deficits were brought about by unsettled obligations left by the previous administration and lack of cash back up turned over.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer, Treasurer and Accountant to exercise prudence in the disbursements for donation to help address the cash deficit the Municipality has been experiencing for years and to formulate policies, procedures and guidelines on the grant of donation/financial assistance considering the need and purpose/nature of the requested assistance.
observations.managementAction: No action had been taken in this recommendation
observations.reason: Inability to trim down financial assistance to constituents in need.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the full utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund for the purpose/projects enumerated under DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.managementAction: Although the Municipal Mayor has instructed the MPDO to closely monitor the implementation of projects under the 20% DF, optimal utilization was not accomplished.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor utilize the regular employees, maximize their worth and potentials instead of hiring consultants. Consultants may be hired only for projects or activities requiring adequate technical and professional expertise that are beyond the optimum in-house capacity and capability as required under the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 and Section 1(a) of CSC Resolution No. 021480 as circularized under Memorandum Circular No. 24 s 2002.
observations.managementAction: No action has been done to implement the recommendation.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Municipal
Accountant and the Acting Property/Supply Officer to continue their efforts to
reconcile and determine the nature of discrepancies noted in the PPE account and
make the necessary adjustments/corrections, whenever applicable.

We also recommended considering all possible remedies to address the negative
observations and problems encountered in the conduct of physical inventory.
Specifically, the Acting Property Officer should ensure that:

2) Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) and/or Property
Acknowledgement Receipt (PAR) are issued for all PPE items issued to actual
recipients and/or end-users and renewed every three years;

b) issued PPE items are returned to the Property Office before the issuance of
Agency Clearance for those officials and employees who will apply for retirement
and/or transfer of office;

¢) demolished constructed buildings, road networks and other infrastructure
assets are inventoried and requested to be dropped from the books;

d) unserviceable equipment which are already disposed are dropped from the
books of account;

e) proper turnover of PPE items from outgoing to incoming officials are
observed/made;

f) donated properties are properly documented and covered by Deeds of
Donation and Acceptance of Donations;

g) extra efforts are exerted to trace records of all PPE facilitate the
reconciliation of PPE accounts.

We further recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Acting
Property Officer to facilitate the disposal of unserviceable motor vehicles and other
properties found unserviceable and see to it that proper procedures were followed
and necessary supporting documents are available for audit before any disposal of
unserviceable property is undertaken.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

o the concerned Municipal official to work out and see to it that Titles of the
purchased and donated parcels of land are transferred in the name of the
Municipal Government of Kawit to establish the Municipality’s absolute
ownership thereof. Coordination by the Municipal Accountant, the
Municipal Assessor, Property Officer and Municipal Treasuarer is necessary
to ensure the correct recording of the parcels of land in the books of
accounts, property records and related reports;

o the Budget Officer to include in the preparation of budget the allocation for
titling of land owned by the Municipality; and

e the Municipal Accountant to book up the parcels of land which were already
transferred to the Municipality by way of donation or deed of sale.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:

a. the Acting Supply Officer to consolidate weekly the RIS using the SSMI and
submit to the Accounting Office for recording of issued supplies and materials;

b. the Municipal Accountant to compute the cost of supplies issued and ending
inventory based on the SSMI and to prepare a journal entry voucher (JEV) to
record expenditures using appropriate expenditures accounts; and

c. the Acting Supply Officer to conduct a complete physical count of all inventory
items regularly at the end of every semester and render a report thereon within
the prescribed deadline and reconcile the records of the Supply Office with the
Accounting records on a per category basis.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal
Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer to closely monitor the cash balance of each
fund and enjoin the Municipal Treasurer from certifying funds availability when no
cash is available to avoid cash deficits. Otherwise, appropriate sanctions maybe
imposed against responsible officials for existence of cash deficits.

We also recommended that trust fund be utilized only for the specific
purpose for which it was intended as required under Section 4(3) of PD No. 1445,

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant look into the difference in
balances of the Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax
Receivables accounts and make the necessary adjustments thereon at their correct
balances.

We also recommended that henceforth, the Accountant shall ensure that
both accounts carry the same balances from the beginning of the year until year
end.

We recommended the Honorable Mayor to direct the HRMO and other
concerned officials to immediately review the job description of each permanent
employee and assess if there is really a need to hire the services of consultants. The
Municipality should maximize the worth of its in-house employees before resorting
to hiring of consultants. Consultants should only be hired for special projects
requiring highly technical and professional expertise as provided by IRR of
Republic Act No. 9184 and Section 1(a) of CSC Resolution No. 021480 as
circularized under Memorandum Circular No. 24 s 2002 for Consultancy Services.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: a. the Inventory Committee conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all assets of the Municipality of Kawit to determine its actual existence and condition and prepare the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment which should incorporate the results of the physical count; b. the OIC-GSO to prepare and properly maintain property cards for each type of PPE and acquired PPE Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to serve as a tool in the reconciliation of records; Moreover, exert effort to trace the whereabouts of donated equipment to other LGUs belonging to the 1% congressional district to enable the transfer of accountabilities thereof. c. the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-GSO to exert effort for the reconciliation of their individual reports and records of PPE. Maintain perpetual records of the municipal properties to prove the reliability of the PPE account.
observations.managementAction:
The Inventory Committee had convened several times and physically counted existing office furniture, fixtures, equipment as well as the transportation equipment belonging to the Municipality, however due to unavailability of other records, other properties were not counted.
The Property Office had prepared the corresponding ARE for all the during the audit period.
The reconciliation of recorded PPE was initiated but still on-going as at the end of the audit period.
Maintain No action had been taken on this recommendation.
observations.reason: The retirement of the previous Property Officer and designation of a new Officer-In-Charge caused delay in the process. Only properties acquired in 2007 onwards were provided with records. No proper turnover of properties from previous administration.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct: o the concerned Municipal official to work out and to see to it that Titles of the purchased and donated parcels of land are transferred in the name of the Municipal Government of Kawit to establish the Municipality’s absolute ownership thereof. Coordination with the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Assessor, Property Officer and Municipal Treasurer is necessary to ensure the correct recording of the parcels of land in the books of accounts, property records and related reports; o the Budget Officer to include in the preparation of budget the allocation for titling of land owned by the Municipality; and o the Municipal Accountant to book up the parcels of land which were already transferred to the Municipality by way of donation or deed of sale.
observations.managementAction: The concerned Municipal officials informed that they have started tracing the pertinent documents relative to the subject municipal properties. Three of the enumerated properties were noted to be road widening in Barangay San Sebastian whose previous owners do not have the Certificate of Title (TCT). Another Property was discovered to have been already titled in the name of Panamitan Elementary School, a government elementary school in Kawit, Cavite. Management officials promised adherence to the audit recommendations to assert their absolute ownership of government properties.
observations.reason: Non-inclusion of the expenses for titing in the Municipality’s Annual Budget during the audit period. Tedious and costly process of land titling hindered the transfer of ownership.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor: a. direct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to immediately undertake in-house appraisal of those unserviceable properties and prepare corresponding report thereof. Submit the NRUP, appraisal report and other required documents to the Auditor concerned for appropriate action, so that disposal of the same could be effected in order to avail of higher economic value when offered at public auction; b. Strictly comply with the policies and procedures on the disposal of property provided in COA Circular No. 92-386. Upon disposal, draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop from the books of accounts the cost of sold/condemned properties.
observations.managementAction:
The Appraisal and Disposal Committee had initiated the in-house appraisal of unserviceable properties and prepare corresponding report thereof. Public auction was conducted in December 5, 2017.
Submitthe NRUP for appraisal report and other required documents to the Auditor concerned for appropriate action. The NRUP for unserviceable properties which were already disposed was already submitted to COA.
observations.reason: Although the necessary proceedings were followed, the Municipal Accountant was not able to drop from the books the cost of disposed properties.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to: b. always see to it that no obligation shall be certified as Accounts Payable, unless these are founded on a valid claim and properly supported by sufficient evidence, in compliance with Section 46 of PD No. 1177. b. immediately analyze the composition of Accounts Payable and revert to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund those found without valid claims and have been outstanding for two years or over.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant has already analyzed the Accounts Payable account during the audit period.
Proper reversion of payables which were found without valid claims had been done during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: a. OIC-BPLO to require the business taxpayers to submit Sworn Statement of all their gross receipts/sales (not only from the Municipal Government of Kawit) for the preceding calendar year and other pertinent records/information for the determination of reliable/accurate gross receipts. b. Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to prepare an updated List of Payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted with the Municipal Government in the preceding year as basis in the assessment of business taxes due. c. OIC - Municipal Treasurer to verify/assess the gross receipts of the nine sampled contractors/suppliers and validate the reported under collection totalling P129,778.00 and enforce the collection of underpayment including penalties and surcharges, if applicable, pursuent to the rates provided in the Municipal Revenue Code. Absence of documented action or information to counter the under collection shall mean issuance of the corresponding Notice of Charge.
observations.managementAction:
Submission of Sworn Statement of all their gross receipts was made as a documentary requirement for securing business/mayor’s permit.
The Municipal Accountant has provided the OIC-Municipal BPLO with the Schedule of Checks issued to contractors/suppliers to serve as basis for computation of business tax to be collected.
Demand letters had been sent by the Municipal Treasurer to contractors/suppliers cooperatively settled their obligations with the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the: BUB Focal Person to closely monitor the implementation of the BUB projects so that optimum goal of inclusive growth and poverty reduction could be fully attained. The Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to immediately refund/remit excess BUB project funds to the source national government agencies to avoid possible loss or misuse.
observations.managementAction:
The BUB focal person has coordinated with the concerned department heads to facilitate implementation of BUB programs/projects.
The Municipal Accountant and the Municipal Treasurer have initiated the refund of excess fund after completion of BUB program/projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local School Board (LSB) to limit the utilization of the SEF budget only to programs, projects and activities defined under the DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 01, 01-A and 01-B to ensure that the purpose for which the fund was established is met. We also recommended to cause the restoration of the amounts inappropriately charged against SEF from the General Fund.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Mayor has instructed the members of the Local School Board to scrutinize the types of programs to be implemented using the SEF as required under DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum Circular Nos. 01, 01-A and 01-B.
We also recommended to cause the restoration of the amounts inappropriately charged against SEF from the General Fund. No action has yet been taken in the recommendation.
observations.reason: No action has yet been taken to restore the amounts inappropriately charged against SEF.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to prevent accumulation of outstanding balances. Further, for long outstanding cash advances, management may avail of the remedies provided under COA Circular No. 97-002 on withholding of salary of the concerned accountable officers.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant was directed to fully adhere to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances. Demand letter had been sent to concerned officers who have long outstanding cash advances so that management can avail of the remedies provided under COA Circular No. 97-002.
Although the necessary proceedings were followed, the subject accountable officers are no longer in service.
observations.reason: The subject accountable officers who have long outstanding cash advances are no longer in service.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer to find ways and means to address the cash deficit the municipality had been experiencing for several years now by exercising diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of government funds.
observations.managementAction: No actions has been taken on the recommendation.
observations.reason: Failure to increase the income/collections of the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor to: utilize the regular employees, maximize their worth and potentials instead of hiring consultants. Consultants may be hired only for projects or activities requiring adequate technical and professional expertise that are beyond the optimum in-house capacity and capability as required under the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 and Section 1(a) of CSC Resolution No. 021480 as circularized under Memorandum Circular No. 24 s 2002; and to ensure prudence in the hiring of consultants by making sure that there are no duplications in their functions.
observations.managementAction:
No action gas been undertaken to implement the recommendation.
No action has been undertaken to implement the recommendation.
observations.reason:
Improper/unsystematic programming of works in some office.
Lack of formulated guidelines in hiring of consultants.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer, Treasurer and Accountant to exercise prudence in the disbursements for donation to help address the cash deficit the Municipality has experiencing for years and to formulate policies, procedures and guidelines on the grant of donation/financial assistance considering the need and purpose/nature of the requested assistance.
observations.managementAction: No action has been undertaken to implement the recommendation.
observations.reason: Lack of formulated policies and guidelines re: granting of financial assistance.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

the Inventory Committee conduct a comprehensive physical inventory of all assets of the Municipality of Kawit to determine its actual existence and condition and prepare the Report of Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment which should incorporate the results of the physical count.
the OIC-GSO to prepare and properly maintain property cards for each type of PPE and Acknowledgement Receipt for Equipment (ARE) to serve as a tool in the reconciliation of records.  Moreover, exert effort to trace the whereabouts of donated equipment to other LGUs belonging to the 1st congressional district to enable the transfer of accountabilities thereof.
the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-GSO to exert effort for the reconciliation of their individual reports and records of PPE.  Maintain perpetual records of the municipal properties to prove the reliability of the PPE account.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct:

the concerned Municipal official to work out and to see to it that Titles of the purchased and donated parcels of land are transferred in the name of the Municipal Government of Kawit to establish the Municipality’s absolute ownership thereof. Coordination with the Municipal Accountant, the Municipal Assessor General Services Officer and Municipal Treasurer is necessary to ensure the correct recording of the parcels of land in the books of accounts, property records and related reports; and
the Municipal Accountant to book up the Land  which were already transferred to the Municipality of by way of donation or deed of sale.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor:

direct the Appraisal and Disposal Committee to immediately undertake in-house appraisal of those unserviceable properties and prepare corresponding report thereof.  Submit the IIRUP, appraisal report and other required documents to the Auditor concerned for appropriate action, so that disposal of the same could be effected in order to avail of higher economic value when offered at public auction.
Strictly comply with the policies and procedures on the disposal of property provided in COA Circular No. 92-386.  Upon disposal, draw a Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) to drop from the books of accounts the sold/condemned properties.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to:

always see to it that no obligation shall be certified as Accounts Payable, unless these are  founded on a valid claim and properly supported by sufficient evidence, in compliance with Section 46 of PD No. 1177.
immediately analyze the composition of Accounts Payables and cause the reversion to the unappropriated surplus of the General Fund those found without valid claims and have been outstanding for two years or over.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer find ways and means to address the cash deficits the municipality had been experiencing for several years now by exercising diligence, prudence and proper control in the disposition of government funds.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

OIC - BPLO to require the business taxpayers to submit Sworn Statement of all their gross receipts/sales (not only from the Municipal Government of Kawit) for the preceding calendar year and other pertinent records/information for the determination of reliable/accurate gross receipts.
Municipal Accountant and the OIC- Municipal Treasurer to prepare an updated List of Payments made to contractors/suppliers who transacted with the Municipal Government in the preceding calendar year to serve as one of the bases in the assessment of business taxes due.
OIC - Municipal Treasurer to verify/assess the gross receipts of the nine sampled contractors/suppliers and validate the reported under collection totaling P129,778.00 and enforce the collection of underpayment including penalties and surcharges, if applicable, pursuant to the rates provided in the Municipal Revenue Code.  Absence of documented action or information to counter the under collection shall mean issuance of the corresponding Notice of Charge.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Budget Officer, Treasurer and Accountant to exercise prudence in the disbursements for donation to help address the cash deficit the Municipality has experiencing for years and to  formulate policies, procedures and guidelines on the grant of donation/financial assistance considering the need and purpose/nature of the requested assistance.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the:

BUB Focal Person to closely monitor the implementation of the BUB projects so that optimum goal of inclusive growth and poverty reduction could be fully attained.
Municipal Accountant and the OIC-Municipal Treasurer to immediately refund/remit excess BUB project funds to the source national government agencies to avoid possible loss or misuse.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the full utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund for the purpose/projects enumerated under DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor utilize the regular employees, maximize their worth and potentials instead of hiring consultants. Consultants may be hired only for projects or activities requiring adequate technical and professional expertise that are beyond the optimum in-house capacity and capability as required under the Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations of RA 9184 and Section 1(a) of CSC Resolution No. 021480 as circularized under Memorandum Circular No. 24 s 2002 for Consultancy Services.

We also recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure prudence in the hiring of consultants by making sure that there are no duplications in their functions.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor direct the Local School Board (LSB) to limit the utilization of the SEF budget only to programs, projects and activities defines under the DECS-DBM-DILG Joint Memorandum /circular Nos. 01, 01-A and 01-B to ensure that the purpose for which the fund was established is met.  We also recommend to cause the restoration of the amounts inappropriately charged against SEF from the General Fund.

We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to prevent accumulation of outstanding balances. Further, we also recommend that for long outstanding cash advances, management may avail of the remedies provided under COA Circular No. 97-002 on withholding of salary of the concerned accountable officers.
We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require:
observations.managementAction:
The Accounting Office has started the analysis of the PPE Accounts.
The newly organized Inventory Committee has conducted the actual inventory taking of the furniture, fixtures and equipment of the Municipality and has prepared the corresponding RCPPE submitted to COA in 2017.
Designation of additional accounting staff to assist in handling of the PPE accounts of the Municipality.
observations.reason:
Insufficient data re: Properties acquired by previous administrations.
