Inability of the Inventory Committee to conduct proper inventory of existing buildings owned by the Municipality due to insufficient property records maintained in the GSO.
Failure to retrieve records as there was no proper turn over from previous administrations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct:
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant has started gathering of data re: Land owned by the municipality.
The Municipal Assessor has prepared the List of Land owned to be used as basis of reconciliation between their records and that of the Municipal Accounting Property Ledger Cards.
observations.reason:
Insufficient office staff to assist in the establishment of full details re: Land account.
Inability to complete the process of reconciliation due to voluminous works obtaining in the accounting office.

observations.managementAction:
The OIC-GSO has prepared the IIRUP for all unserviceable office furniture and equipment junked in their storeroom.
The reorganized Inventory, Appraisal and Disposal Committee has convened to determine the appraised value of unserviceable properties and the mode of disposing thereof.
No action was yet taken by the Management.
No action was undertaken to implement the recommendation.
observations.reason:
Failure to complete the process due to time constraint and insufficient trainings of designated members of the committee.
Time constraint as the committee was only reorganized during the latter part of the year.
Absence of Appraisal report and other documentary requirements.
Insufficient documents to support the dropping from the books of accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant:
observations.managementAction: Additional Accounting staff was designated who worked on providing depreciation for each PPE and prepared the individual PPE Ledger Cards for each type of property owned by the Municipality.

observations.recommendation: to reconcile the Motor Vehicles accounts per General Ledger with that of per Schedule of Depreciation of PPE and make the necessary adjustment in the books.
observations.managementAction: Reconciliation of Motor Vehicles account has been done thus corresponding depreciation had been provided.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to verify and address the differences that exist between the amounts set-up for Real Property Tax Receivables and Special Education Tax Receivables as well as their respective collections and make the necessary adjustments; and to ensure the equality of amounts set-up and collected for Real Property Tax and Special Education Tax Receivables.
observations.managementAction:
The Municipal Accountant has verified and addressed the differences between the amount previously set-up for RPT and SET receivables.
Equality of amounts set-up for RPT and SET has been properly observed during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to immediately analyze the composition of Accounts Payables, investigate the cause of negative balances, identify the items “To be distributed” and to come up with a reliable balance.
observations.managementAction: The Accounting official in-charge of the account has fully analyzed the composition of the Accounts Payable and adjustments had been made to correct the balance of the account.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to immediately exert effort in analyzing the composition of Other Payables and to come up with a reliable balance.
observations.managementAction: Analysis of the Other Payables account was made during the year and corresponding adjustments have been effected.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant: to provide reliable supporting schedules to Due to BIR by indicating the Tax Identification Number of the creditors, investigating accounts with negative balances, identifying the composition of the items “to be distributed”, “general payroll” and “Due to BIR”; and to see to it that taxes withheld are remitted to Bureau of Internal Revenue within the prescribed period to avoid the incurrence of penalties and surcharges imposed on late remittances.
observations.managementAction:
Supporting schedules for the account Due to BIR has been prepared.
Most of the taxes withheld during the year were immediately remitted to the BIR.
observations.reason: Insufficient cash back up to support full remittance of prior years’ unremitted balances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct: the Municipal Accountant to review, analyze, validate and reconcile the dormant accounts and to prepare the necessary adjusting entries; and the Municipal Accountant, guided by the procedures in writing off dormant accounts, initiate actions towards requesting to the Sangguniang Bayan for the passage of a Resolution requesting authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off the non-moving receivables.
observations.managementAction:
Request for writing off of dormant accounts has been given priority.
The Municipal Accountant, guided by the procedures in writing off dormant accounts, initiate actions towards requesting to the Sangguniang Bayan for the passage of a Resolution requesting authority from the Commission on Audit to write-off the non-moving receivables.
observations.reason:
Incomplete documents to support the request for write-off.
Inability to complete the documentary requirements for the writing-off of dormant accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant: to exert effort to identify the breakdown and provide complete and accurate information/data supporting the P3Million comprising 86 percent the account Due to Other NGAs in the General Fund; to analyze, reconcile and make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct amount of the account Due to Other NGAs in the Trust Fund Books; and to see to it that transactions are properly analyzed as to what particular account and subsidiary ledger to be used in recording the transactions to avoid presentation of misleading information.
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Accountant has thoroughly analyzed and reconciled schedules of actual unexpended balances as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor instruct the Municipal Accountant to strictly adhere to the guidelines in the granting, utilization and liquidation of cash advances to prevent accumulation of outstanding balances. Further, we also recommend that for long outstanding cash advances, management may avail of the remedies provided under COA Circular No. 97-002 on withholding of salary of the concerned accountable officers.
observations.managementAction: Quarterly monitoring of cash advances and liquidations has been made.
observations.reason: Prior year’s cash advances totaling P86,400.00 remained unliquidated due to inability to locate the whereabouts of the accountable officials.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require the Municipal Accountant to review the Journal Entry Voucher being drawn by her staff and ensure the equality in the total balances of the Due from Other Funds and Due to Other Funds accounts in all funds being maintained by the Municipality.  Any difference should be immediately analyzed and adjusted accordingly.
observations.managementAction: An Accounting staff was designated to focus on the analysis of the accounts, thus equality of the amounts has been observed during the year.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor ensure the full utilization of the 20 percent Development Fund for the purpose/projects enumerated under DILG and DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1.
observations.managementAction: A focal person was designated by the Municipal Mayor to closely monitor the implementation of the programs under the 20% Development Fund.
observations.reason: Lack of adequate personnel in the Municipal Engineer’s Office to assist in the timely preparation of program of works.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Mayor require: the Local Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the preparation of the 10-year solid waste management plan by the Municipal Solid Waste Management Board and have it approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission to ensure that the required efficient solid waste management is met; the implementation of the mandatory Segregation of Solid Waste at source and promote awareness of the constituents through information campaign in every barangay; and the establishment of functional Material Recovery Facility in every barangay or cluster of barangays.
observations.managementAction:
The 10-year solid waste management plan has been prepared and submitted and was already approved by the National Solid Waste Management Commission.  Copy of the plan was provided to the COA Office.
Information campaign has been undertaken in every barangays for the implementation of the mandatory segregation of solid waste at source.
The MENRO, MPDC, Barangay Officials and the SWM Council have worked out for the MRF establishment.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the OIC, MDRRMC to include in the preparation of the LDRRMF Investment Plan for each year the unexpended balance of the LDRRMF of previous years, to serve as the guide in the utilization of the LDRRMF; and the Municipal Accountant see to it that continuing appropriations of the LDRRMF is utilized only for capital outlay and be guided by the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 in the utilization of the Fund.
observations.managementAction:
The LDRRMF Investment Plan for the succeeding year has already included the unexpended balance of previous years.
The Municipal Accountant has properly observed the utilization of the LDRRMF for capital outlay purposes only.
